title: HTTP Semantics, Messages, Versions, and the Wire Model · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs http semantics, messages, versions, and the wire model from bounded byte and state evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the version-neutral message and semantics model. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for version-neutral message and semantics model after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate version-neutral method, status, field, content, and resource semantics from HTTP/1.1 textual serialization.
Model control data, header fields, content, trailer fields, and message metadata without inventing content where semantics forbid it.
Determine content meaning and permission from request method, response status, request context, and connection mode rather than one field alone.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Semantics Syntax Separation · changed case · Review Semantics Syntax Separation
steps.instruction:
review case — A review protocol team handles case 3983 in a signed webhook receiver behind an HTTP/2 to HTTP/1.1 gateway. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive message content from method and status semantics before wire framing; preserve this operating constraint: a timeout must reserve cleanup time and retain the terminal phase; then inject Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length and close before any downstream forwarding. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate version-neutral method, status, field, content, and resource semantics from HTTP/1.1 textual serialization.
